definition: Father of the Hebrew nation. Originally called Abram, God renamed him Abraham ('father of many nations'). He was called by God to leave Ur of the Chaldees and journey to Canaan. His faith was tested when God asked him to sacrifice his son Isaac.

The great Hebrew leader, lawgiver, and prophet who led the Israelites out of Egyptian slavery. He received the Ten Commandments on Mount Sinai and authored the first five books of the Bible (the Pentateuch).
The second king of Israel, a shepherd boy who defeated Goliath. Known as 'a man after God's own heart,' he united Israel and established Jerusalem as the capital. Author of many Psalms.
Son of David and third king of Israel, renowned for his wisdom. He built the first Temple in Jerusalem. Author of Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, and Song of Solomon.
The first man, created by God from the dust of the ground. God breathed life into him and placed him in the Garden of Eden. He is the ancestor of all humanity.
The first woman, made by God from Adam's rib. She was deceived by the serpent in the Garden of Eden and ate the forbidden fruit. She is called 'the mother of all living.'
A righteous man who found favor with God. God commanded him to build an ark to save his family and pairs of all animals from the great flood that destroyed the earth.
Son of Isaac and Rebekah, twin brother of Esau. He wrestled with God and was renamed Israel. Father of twelve sons who became the twelve tribes of Israel.
Favorite son of Jacob, sold into slavery by his jealous brothers. He rose to become second-in-command of Egypt and saved his family during a great famine.
One of the greatest Old Testament prophets, often called the 'Messianic Prophet' for his detailed prophecies about the coming Messiah, including the Suffering Servant passages.
A powerful prophet of Israel who confronted King Ahab and the prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel. He was taken up to heaven in a chariot of fire without experiencing death.
A Hebrew exile in Babylon known for his faithfulness to God. He interpreted dreams, survived the lions' den, and received apocalyptic visions about future kingdoms.

Originally Saul of Tarsus, a Pharisee who persecuted Christians. After a dramatic conversion on the road to Damascus, he became the greatest missionary of the early church and authored many New Testament epistles.
One of the twelve apostles, originally named Simon. Jesus called him 'the rock' (Petros). He was a fisherman who became a leader of the early church and preached the first sermon at Pentecost.
The mother of Jesus, a young virgin from Nazareth. The angel Gabriel announced to her that she would conceive by the Holy Spirit and bear the Son of God.
The central figure of Christianity, believed to be the Son of God and the promised Messiah. Born in Bethlehem, he taught, performed miracles, was crucified, and rose from the dead.
One of the twelve apostles, known as 'the disciple whom Jesus loved.' Son of Zebedee, he authored the Gospel of John, three epistles, and the Book of Revelation.
A Moabite woman who chose to follow her mother-in-law Naomi and the God of Israel after her husband's death. She married Boaz and became an ancestor of King David and Jesus.
A Jewish queen of Persia who risked her life to save her people from a plot of extermination devised by Haman. Her courage is commemorated in the festival of Purim.
Known as the 'weeping prophet,' he prophesied during the fall of Jerusalem. He warned of coming judgment while also prophesying a new covenant that God would make with His people.
The last judge and first major prophet of Israel. Dedicated to God by his mother Hannah before birth, he anointed both Saul and David as kings of Israel.
Moses' successor who led the Israelites into the Promised Land. He commanded the conquest of Canaan, beginning with the fall of Jericho.
A reluctant prophet whom God sent to preach repentance to Nineveh. He tried to flee by ship but was swallowed by a great fish for three days before obeying God's command.
A wealthy and righteous man from the land of Uz. God allowed Satan to test his faith through severe suffering and loss. Despite his trials, Job maintained his integrity before God.
Wife of Abraham, originally named Sarai. Despite being barren for many years, God promised she would bear a son. She gave birth to Isaac at age 90.
